items.label: Getting Started
items.content:
## Welcome!

**Welcome to ZYNQ!**

This library provides hundreds of games with rich metadata and thumbnails, alongside a separate WebRTC-powered social system for real-time interaction.

Choose your preferred way to install the library below.

items.label: Script Tag
items.content:
### Script Tag

Use this if you want the fastest setup via CDN.

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.0.8/index.min.js"></script>
```

items.label: ES Module
items.content:
### ES Module

For modern JavaScript projects (Vite, Webpack, etc).

```javascript
import { games, peer } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.0.8/index.min.js"
```

items.label: Game Object
items.content:
## ZYNQ.games
### Game Object

A game object returned by `ZYNQ.games` methods.

```text
Game:
- name: string          // Display name of the game
- category?: string     // Optional category (e.g. "shooter", "platformer")
- alias: string         // Unique identifier used in URLs and history
- url: string           // Full playable URL
- thumb: string         // Thumbnail image URL
- devices?: number[]    // Supported device types (for filtering)

Computed properties:
- isSupported: boolean  // True if the game runs on the current device
- isFavorite: boolean   // True if the user marked it as favorite
```

children.label: list
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.list

The current filtered and/or sorted array of games. This is what you usually display in your UI.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.list
```

children.label: total
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.total

Total number of games loaded in the library.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.total
```

children.label: filteredCount
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.filteredCount

Number of games currently visible after search, filters, or sorting.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filteredCount
```

children.label: history
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.history

Array of recently played game aliases (max 50, persisted in localStorage).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.history
```

children.label: favorites
children.content:
### ZYNQ.games.favorites

Set of favorite game aliases (persisted in localStorage).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.favorites
```

children.label: .init()
children.content:
### init()

Initialize the game system and load the game database. Call this first.

```javascript
await ZYNQ.games.init()
```

children.label: .search()
children.content:
### search(query)

Filter games by name or alias. Returns the `ZYNQ.games` object for chaining.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.search("alien")
```

children.label: .random()
children.content:
### random(count = 1)

Get a random selection of games and update the filtered list.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.random(5)
```

children.label: .getRandomOne()
children.content:
### getRandomOne()

Return a single random game object (does not change the filtered list).

```javascript
const game = ZYNQ.games.getRandomOne()
```

children.label: .shuffle()
children.content:
### shuffle()

Randomly shuffle the current filtered list.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.shuffle()
```

children.label: .sortBy()
children.content:
### sortBy(field = "name")

Sort the current filtered list by a property (e.g. "name").

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.sortBy("name")
```

children.label: .sortByPopularity()
children.content:
### sortByPopularity()

Sort games by popularity score (if available in the data).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.sortByPopularity()
```

children.label: .getByAlias()
children.content:
### getByAlias(alias)

Find and return a single game by its alias.

```javascript
const game = ZYNQ.games.getByAlias("alien-hominid")
```

children.label: .getByCategory()
children.content:
### getByCategory(category)

Filter games by category and update the list.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.getByCategory("shooter")
```

children.label: .filterSupported()
children.content:
### filterSupported()

Keep only games supported on the current device.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filterSupported()
```

children.label: .filterFavorites()
children.content:
### filterFavorites()

Keep only the user's favorite games.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filterFavorites()
```

children.label: .toggleFavorite()
children.content:
### toggleFavorite(alias)

Add or remove a game from favorites (persisted automatically).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.toggleFavorite("alien-hominid")
```

items.label: Example
items.content:
### Games Example

```javascript
await ZYNQ.games.init()

const results = ZYNQ.games
  .search("alien")
  .filterSupported()
  .sortBy("name")
  .list

console.log(results)
```

items.label: Constructor
items.content:
## ZYNQ.peer

### Creating a Peer Instance

ZYNQ uses a wrapper around **PeerJS** for easy WebRTC peer-to-peer connections (text chat, video & audio).

You can create an instance in two ways:

1. `new ZYNQ.peer(options)` ŌĆö for full control
2. `ZYNQPeer.get(remoteId, options)` ŌĆö singleton per remote peer (recommended for simple use)

```javascript
new ZYNQ.peer(options)
```

items.label: Options
items.content:
### Constructor Options

These options configure what the peer supports and how it behaves.

- **video**: boolean ŌĆö Enable video stream (default: true)
- **audio**: boolean ŌĆö Enable audio stream (default: true)
- **txt**: boolean ŌĆö Enable text/data messaging (default: true)
- **debug**: boolean ŌĆö Enable extra logging (default: false)
- **rateLimit**: number ŌĆö Max messages per second (default: 30)
- **maxMsgSize**: number ŌĆö Maximum size of a text message in bytes (default: 65536)
- **autoReconnect**: boolean ŌĆö Automatically try to reconnect on disconnect (default: true)
- **maxReconnectAttempts**: number ŌĆö How many times to retry (default: 5)
- **id**: string | null ŌĆö Optional custom peer ID

Example:
```javascript
const peer = new ZYNQ.peer({ video: true, audio: true, txt: true })
```

children.label: .connect()
children.content:
### connect(remoteId)

Start a connection to another peer. This sets up both data (text) and media (video/audio) connections if enabled.

```javascript
peer.connect("other-peer-id")
```

children.label: .send()
children.content:
### send(data)

Send text or any serializable data to the connected peer. Rate-limited and size-checked automatically.

```javascript
peer.send("Hello ­¤æĮ")
peer.send({ type: "chat", message: "Hi there!" })
```

children.label: .getStream()
children.content:
### getStream()

Request access to the local camera and/or microphone. Returns a MediaStream.

```javascript
const stream = await peer.getStream()
```

children.label: .close()
children.content:
### close()

Close the current connection gracefully. Does not destroy the peer.

```javascript
peer.close()
```

children.label: .destroy()
children.content:
### destroy()

Fully destroy the peer, close all connections, and clean up resources.

```javascript
peer.destroy()
```

children.label: open / ready
children.content:
### open / ready event

Fired when the peer successfully connects to the signaling server and receives its own ID.

```javascript
peer.on("open", (id) => {
  console.log("My Peer ID:", id)
})
```

children.label: data
children.content:
### data event

Fired when text or data is received from the remote peer.

```javascript
peer.on("data", (message) => {
  console.log("Received:", message)
})
```

children.label: stream
children.content:
### stream event

Fired when a remote video/audio stream is received.

```javascript
peer.on("stream", (remoteStream) => {
  videoElement.srcObject = remoteStream
})
```

children.label: close
children.content:
### close event

Fired when the connection to the remote peer is closed.

```javascript
peer.on("close", () => {
  console.log("Connection closed")
})
```

children.label: error
children.content:
### error event

Fired when something goes wrong (connection error, media error, etc.).

```javascript
peer.on("error", (err) => {
  console.error("Peer error:", err)
})
```

children.label: reconnecting
children.content:
### reconnecting event

Fired when auto-reconnect is attempted (includes attempt number and delay).

```javascript
peer.on("reconnecting", ({ attempt, delay }) => {
  console.log(`Reconnecting attempt ${attempt} in ${delay}ms`)
})
```

children.label: localStream

items.label: Example
